title: Lab 04: SELinux Custom HTTP Port
description: Fix Apache so it listens on a nonstandard port without disabling SELinux

tasks: Configure Apache on clientvm so it listens on TCP port 9082 and starts automatically at boot.
Allow TCP port 9082 through the firewall permanently.
Make the SELinux changes needed so Apache serves the existing /var/www/html content on that port.
hints: Do not move the existing document root.
checks: grep -Eq '^Listen 9082$' /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled
curl -fsS http://localhost:9082 >/dev/null && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active && semanage port -l | grep -Eq '^http_port_t\b.*\b9082\b'
task_titles: Configure Apache on clientvm so it listens on TCP...
Allow TCP port 9082 through the firewall permanently
Make the SELinux changes needed so Apache serves...
